brand.name: None
brand.vertical: unknown (placeholder record — none.com serves a blank page)

summary: No verifiable information exists for this brand: none.com returns a completely blank page and web searches surface nothing. This is a placeholder/test record, so the campaigns below follow the playbook's default priority order for a non-ecom brand rather than brand-specific signals.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Drive activation — get a new contact to first value (the 'aha' moment) as fast as possible.
campaigns.trigger: Signup / account created / list join — send within seconds of the event.
campaigns.segment: All new contacts; branch on signup-survey answer (goal, role, or use case) once that data exists.
campaigns.rationale: Welcome is the highest-engagement send and fastest path to proving value, so it is the default first build for any new non-ecom brand — especially one with no existing lifecycle to evaluate.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome, set expectations for the sequence, and deliver one quick first win with a single primary action.
emails.subject: Welcome to None — your first win is inside
emails.preview: Two minutes to the outcome you came here for.
emails.cta: Do your single most important first action
keyPoints: Thank them for joining and reassure them they're in the right place
Point to one specific first action that produces a fast, concrete payoff
Set expectations: a few short, no-fluff emails over the next two weeks
Invite a real reply — a human reads every one

campaigns.name: Trial / Consultation-to-Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert a free trial, freemium user, or free consultation into a paying customer.
campaigns.trigger: Trial start / freemium signup / free-consult booked, with behavioral milestones and an expiry countdown running in parallel.
campaigns.segment: Active trials or booked consults; branch by usage depth (activated vs. stalled).
campaigns.rationale: Conversion is the core revenue play and the standard second build wherever a trial, demo, or consult motion exists — it turns the activation from Campaign 1 into paid customers.

emails.purpose: Expiry warning that frames what they stand to lose and lays out a clear, low-friction upgrade path plus an offer of help.
emails.subject: Your None trial ends Friday — keep what you've built
emails.preview: Upgrade in one click, or grab time with us first.
keyPoints: State the exact expiry date and what access/results end with it
Recap the value they've already gotten during the trial
Give one obvious upgrade button plus a human-contact fallback
Address the top objection (price/risk) in one line

campaigns.name: Booking, Reminders & No-Show Recovery
campaigns.objective: Maximize attended meetings/appointments and recover the ones that slip.
campaigns.trigger: Booking confirmed; upcoming appointment; no-show event.
campaigns.segment: Anyone with a scheduled meeting, demo, consult, or appointment.
campaigns.rationale: If the brand runs on appointments or demos, reminders recover revenue immediately (~35% fewer no-shows on average) — the cheapest, fastest ROI win after onboarding and conversion.

emails.purpose: The 24-hour-before reminder — the single highest-leverage send for cutting no-shows — with prep and a frictionless reschedule link.
emails.subject: See you tomorrow at None — quick prep inside
emails.preview: Here's the time, the link, and what to have ready.
keyPoints: Confirm the exact date, time, and join link or address
Tell them what to bring or prepare so the session is productive
Offer a one-click reschedule to reduce silent no-shows
Name the rep/practitioner they'll be meeting

campaigns.name: Lead-Magnet Nurture
campaigns.objective: Move a cold lead toward sales-readiness over a long cycle without discounting.
campaigns.trigger: Gated-content download (guide, checklist, webinar, ROI calculator, or free tool).
campaigns.segment: Leads below the sales-ready lead-score threshold; branch by the topic of the magnet they downloaded.
campaigns.rationale: Once a lead magnet feeds the top of funnel, this always-on nurture compounds — a comparable B2B sequence beat the client's newsletter by +650% CTR and +32.6% conversion.

emails.purpose: Deliver the downloaded asset instantly and reframe the real problem it solves to start the buyer-journey at problem recognition.
emails.subject: Your None guide is here — plus the real problem behind it
emails.preview: The download, and the mistake it's actually fixing.
keyPoints: Deliver the promised asset above the fold with a clear link
Frame the underlying problem the asset addresses
Set up the next emails as a short, useful series
Keep it value-first — no pitch yet

campaigns.name: Win-Back / Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate dormant contacts and churned customers, and clean the list to protect deliverability.
campaigns.trigger: Inactivity threshold (e.g., 60–90 days with no opens/clicks/logins) or cancellation.
campaigns.segment: Dormant subscribers; separately, churned customers get a different message.
campaigns.rationale: Win-back is quick to ship, recovers 5–15% of inactive contacts, and protects sender reputation by sunsetting non-responders — a low-effort, high-protection addition to the core flows.

emails.purpose: Open the re-engagement sequence with a 'we miss you' that reminds the contact of the core value and invites them back with low friction.
emails.subject: Did we lose you?
emails.preview: Still want what brought you to None? One click back in.
emails.cta: Pick up where you left off
keyPoints: Acknowledge the gap warmly, without guilt
Restate the core value/outcome they originally wanted
Make returning a single, obvious action
Signal that the next emails will show what's new
